alt_forms.note: Classical Japanese term for armed samurai-era warriors; implies historical Japanese martial culture specifically.

definitions.gloss_en: a warrior or combat-capable unit type
definitions.gloss_jp: {戦|せん}{闘|とう}を{行|おこな}う{人|ひと}または{ユニット|ゆにっと}
definitions.explanation_en: A generic label for a frontline fighting unit or character class in games. In wargames, used as a unit-type designation; in TRPGs, often a character class name.
definitions.implicit_notes: Carries connotations of melee or close-combat specialization. Does not imply rank or command.
definitions.orthography_notes: Native Japanese compound: 戦 (war/battle) + 士 (person of skill/rank). Not a Warhammer coinage; predates modern miniature wargames. Kotobank and Weblio list it as a general dictionary term.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Broad game-setting applicability. In TRPGs (D&D Japanese translation, Hobby Japan), 戦士 is a character class name. In miniature wargames, used as a unit type. 武者 is preferred in games with explicitly Japanese historical settings.

usage_jp: {戦|せん}{士|し}ユニット (warrior unit); {戦|せん}{士|し}クラス (warrior class)
usage_en: "warrior", "fighter", or "soldier" depending on context. Use "warrior" when heroic or classlike; "soldier" when military-regimented.
comparative_notes: 武者 (musha) is the classical samurai-era cognate but is domain-specific. 兵士 (heishi, soldier) implies military rank structure and is less common as a unit type name.

cross_references.note: 攻撃 — the primary action of a 戦士 unit
